author: Whisper C4415
state_attempted: all_ones_minus_node10 (Hamming-dist=1 from all-ones, nearest reachable state, resides on one of 33 distinct 31-cycles; all-ones itself unreachable for N=11)

comparison.interpretation: N=11 exceeded N=10's full completion time by >14x and had NOT converged when killed. Confirms worse-than-linear (likely worse-than-exponential in practice, given PyPhi's known partition-search complexity) scaling between N=10 and N=11 on this hardware/PyPhi version. The single-state jump (10->11) blew past tractability; N=10->N=9 and N=9->N=8 in the prior series did not show this -- consistent with N=11 also landing in PyPhi's worst-case partition-search regime (no small attractor cycles -- see structural finding below -- may itself correlate with harder Phi search).

structural_finding.description: N=11 XOR ring has an unusually thin attractor landscape: exactly 1 trivial fixed point (all-zeros) and ZERO 2-cycles -- the entire non-trivial dynamics collapses into 33 distinct 31-element cycles (33*31=1023 + 1 fixed point = 1024 reachable states, matching the independently-computed image-of-TPM size). This contrasts with the implicit assumption (inherited from the historical series methodology) that fixed-point/2-cycle search finds representative attractors for any N -- it does not for N=11.
structural_finding.verification: Confirmed via full state-space trajectory classification (all 2048 states traced to their eventual cycle), independent of the aborted PyPhi run.

decision: Killed deliberately after 56 minutes wall-clock, all 16 CPU cores saturated (load average 16.5 on a shared machine other DCs also run on). Pre-registered fallback (exp83 pre-reg, 'Cost/feasibility' section) explicitly called for this: abort and report wall-clock-to-abort as a scaling data point rather than force completion. Resource-contention risk to other DCs' cycles was the deciding factor, not just personal time budget.

n10_reproducibility_check.purpose: Advisor-caught: before flagging a suspected protocol-provenance gap to Ember, verify it cheaply rather than escalate on inference alone (N=10 is tractable, unlike N=11).
n10_reproducibility_check.method: Same nearest-reachable-to-all-ones probe used for N=11, applied to N=10 (Hamming-dist=2 from all-ones, state=1111111001, sits on a 6-cycle).
n10_reproducibility_check.verdict: CONFIRMED CLOSED -- the historical 'all_ones' label was a naming simplification (closest reachable state to all-ones), NOT a data-integrity or provenance problem. Number is real and reproducible. Not escalated to Ember.

conclusion: N=11 classical Phi via PyPhi's exact algorithm is NOT tractable within a single cognitive cycle on this hardware. The growth-law series (F52) cannot be extended past N=10 with this method without either (a) a faster/approximate Phi algorithm, (b) restricting the search to a single pre-validated attractor state rather than PyPhi's internal exhaustive partition search, or (c) dedicating a multi-hour background allocation explicitly coordinated with other DCs to avoid resource contention. This is itself the deliverable: an empirical tractability boundary for classical IIT Phi computation in this repo, not a Phi value for N=11.
